REPORT RELAYED VIA BROADCAST MEDIA OF TREES UPROOTED ALONG EL PORTAL DRIVE AND GOLFVIEW DRIVE IN THE TEQUESTA COUNTRY CLUB. THIS WAS FROM THE SAME STORM THAT EVENTUALLY PRODUCED THE TORNADO/WATERSPOUT ALONG US 1 AND THE INDIAN RIVER ALONG THE COUNTY LINE.
TREES DOWN. TIME DETERMINED BY RADAR.

VIDEO OBTAINED FROM WEST PALM BEACH BROADCAST MEDIA SHOWED A LIKELY TORNADIC CIRCULATION AT THE COUNTY LINE PLAZA IN TEQUESTA. DAMAGE APPEARED TO BE LIMITED TO UPROOTED TREES IN THE PARKING LOT AND ROOFING MATERIAL. THE ROOF AT THE SHOPPING CENTER WAS UNDERGOING REPAIR AT THE TIME OF THE TORNADO, THEREFORE MUCH OF THE ROOFING MATERIAL WAS ALREADY DETACHED FROM THE STRUCTURE. WATER ENTERED A BUSINESS IN THE PLAZA FROM ONE OF THE SECTIONS WHERE THE ROOF WAS BEING REPAIRED.

VIDEO OBTAINED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA DEPICTS WATERSPOUT COMING ASHORE NEAR THE WINDSOR CONDOMINUMS OFF BAY COLONY DRIVE. SEVERAL BRANCHES AND OTHER DEBRIS SEEN FLYING BEFORE TORNADO DISSIPATES LESS THAN 1 MINUTE AFTER REACHING SHORE.

SEVERAL ROOFS BLOWN OFF OF MOBILE HOMES IN THE CAPE SABLE LAKES MOBILE HOME PARK. ADDITIONAL TREES AND BRANCHES WERE DOWNED. SOME DEBRIS WAS BLOWN INTO ADJACENT NEIGHBORHOOD. STREETS IMPACTED INCLUDE CAPE SABLE DRIVE AND CAPE HAYE LANE.

SURVEY BY COLLIER COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT STAFF FOUND ROOF AND CARPORT DAMAGE TO AT LEAST FOUR MOBILE HOMES IN THE CAPE SABLE LAKES COMMUNITY SOUTH OF RADIO ROAD. STREETS IMPACTED INCLUDE CAPE SABLE DRIVE AND CAPE HAYE LANE. DAMAGE PATTERN WAS INCONSISTENT AND THUS HARD TO DETERMINE WITH A HIGH LEVEL OF CONFIDENCE WHETHER IT WAS CAUSED BY A TORNADO. STRUCTURES DAMAGED WERE RATHER WEAK COMPARED TO SURROUNDING UNITS. THEREFORE... DAMAGE BEING CATEGORIZED AS CAUSED BY THUNDERSTORM WIND.
